title: Update tmp dependency to >=0.2.4 to fix vulnerability (GHSA-52f5-9888-hmc6)
author: lgcarlinf
body:
Hello 👋,  

First, thanks for maintaining this project 🙏.  

I noticed that this library depends on **[`tmp`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/tmp)** in a version lower than `0.2.4`. That version has a known vulnerability:  

- **Advisory:** [GHSA-52f5-9888-hmc6](https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-52f5-9888-hmc6)  
- **Severity:** High  
- **Patched in:** `tmp@0.2.4`  

## Steps to reproduce
1. Install this library.  
2. Run `npm audit` / `pnpm audit` / `yarn audit`.  
3. Observe the warning about the vulnerable `tmp` dependency.  

## Suggested fix
Bump the `tmp` dependency to at least **`0.2.4`** to ensure downstream users are not exposed to this vulnerability.  

Thanks again for your work on this project 🙌.

comments.author: TamanRise
comments.body:
Hi 👋,

Just wanted to add that we're also facing the same issue in our project. We've been alerted about the tmp vulnerability (GHSA-52f5-9888-hmc6) and are seeing the same warning in our npm audit reports. It’s affecting us when using exceljs@4.4.0 due to the older version of tmp being included.

I second the suggestion to bump tmp to at least 0.2.4 to avoid exposing downstream users to this vulnerability.

Thanks for the hard work on this project!

comments.author: Silentscripter
comments.body: FYI updating tmp to v >= 0.2.4 is causing this bug as side effect when using the strem reader https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/issues/683

comments.author: gameroman
comments.body:
Hi, I've forked this into https://github.com/excel-js/exceljs if someone is interested
